rulings.Case Title: EMILIO SAENZ et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 13, 2009, line 16, DEFENDANT DAP, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment denied. Triable issue of fact raised regarding exposure. (Plaintiff's declaration, Ay declaration, Defendant's answers to interrogatories.) Defendant's objection to plaintiff's declaration overruled. Plaintiff's objections to Treat declaration sustained as to paragraphs 5, 9, and 10, remaining objections overruled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 530 JACKSON STREET, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 13, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Sufficient primary insurance is available to plaintiff to satisfy the unpaid balence of plaintiff's stipulated judgment. The excess liability policies at issue are not triggered, plaintiff has no duty to recover on them, and defendant has no duty to defend. The "underlying insurance" is not just that specifically listed in the underlying policy, but "any other insurance availbe to the insured..." The "underlying limits" includes availability to the insured irrespective of Bankruptcy or insolvency of any insurer. (See Exhibit E. )=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE A. MCFADDEN VS. MARY M. CHIARENZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 13, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT MARY CHIARENZA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts showing that his reliance on defendant's misrepresentations caused him to incur certain costs and expenses other than the lost opportunity to insist upon security for defendant's debt. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MOHSAN KIRIMI VS. SHAHNAWAZ MANJI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 13, 2009, line 17, DEFENDANT SHAHNAWAZ MANJI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten day's leave to amend for first cause of action for conversion. Plaintiff to allege facts to clarify the down payment discrepency and specify why he had the right to possess the store. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend third cause of action for breach of fidiciary duty that is not applicable to seller/buyer transactions. (Comm. on Childeren's TV vs. General Foods (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197.) Demurrer overruled as to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract, issues to be raised in answer. Damages alleged appropriately. =(302/CWW)
